name: basic-ecom-backend
description: This is the backend microservice of the toy ecommerce application I am building.This is a project to help myself learn how backend works using NodeJS.
author: Bibhu Prasad Rout
x_learningNotes: This file is package.json, the Node.js project manifest. It is pure JSON, so it cannot contain comments like // or /* */. To keep learning explanations inside the file without breaking npm, we store them under a custom field that npm ignores.
"name" and "version" identify your package. "private" is not set here, so publishing is technically possible; many apps set "private": true to prevent accidental publish.
"main": "src/app.js" is the entry file when this package is required by another Node program. For an application, it also acts as a documentation hint: the server starts at src/app.js.
"scripts" are named commands you run via npm. "start" runs node directly. "dev" uses nodemon to restart on changes and cross-env to set NODE_ENV in a cross-platform way (Windows vs Unix).

"dependencies" are runtime packages required for the server to function (express, mongoose, jsonwebtoken, bcrypt, etc.). "devDependencies" are tools used during development (nodemon, cross-env). In production installs, devDependencies are often skipped.
Semver (^) means "compatible updates". For example "express": "^4.21.2" allows updates like 4.22.0 but not 5.0.0. This balances getting bugfixes with avoiding breaking changes.
If you add linting/testing tooling later (eslint, prettier, jest config), it commonly appears here as devDependencies and additional scripts.
